message:
fix(web): prevent SSE data field injection via bare carriage return

The SSE data serializer split on \n only, but the WHATWG SSE spec
defines three line terminators: \r\n, \r, and \n. A bare \r in event
data would be interpreted by the browser's EventSource parser as a
line terminator, allowing injection of arbitrary SSE fields (retry:,
event:, id:) by an attacker who controls part of the data string.

Example: data="hello\rretry:999999999" would produce a single
data: line on the wire, but the browser would parse it as:
  data:hello
  retry:999999999

Fix: normalize \r\n → \n and bare \r → \n before splitting, matching
the behavior already used for the comment field (.lines()).

The event: and id: fields were already safe (strip \r and \n).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
